string.text:
The Type CQ is an unlicensed Chinese variant of the M16 rifle first introduced in the early 1980s by Sichuan Changqing Machine Works, a subsidiary of Norinco, and intended for export sales. It is a a gas-operated, rotating-bolt full-automatic firearm chambered for 5.56?45mm. and this CQ 5.56 variant, also known as the CQ-311 or CQ M-311, is a select-fire assault rifle version intended for military and police use.
 Though it has the same look as the M16 rifle, it has a different rifling pitch intended to stabilize the Type CJ Chinese clone of the 5.56?45mm NATO cartride, but it will not optimally stabilize the NATO standard 5.56mm ammunition.
 
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS:
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Suppressor: NATO 5.56 Suppressor
